species: Candidatus Alkanophaga (archaea; MAGs Ca. Alkanophaga volatiphilum / Ca. Alkanophaga liquidiphilum) in syntrophic sulfate-reducing consortium with Candidatus Thermodesulfobacterium syntrophicum

growth_records.strain: Enrichment culture from Guaymas Basin hydrothermal vent sediment (MAG4 = Ca. Alkanophaga volatiphilum, plus a second Ca. Alkanophaga MAG); not axenic
growth_records.medium: Synthetic sulfate-reducer medium (SRM) with petroleum alkanes (C5-C14) as sole carbon/electron source and sulfate as electron acceptor
growth_records.cite: Zehnle et al. Candidatus Alkanophaga archaea from Guaymas Basin hydrothermal vent sediment oxidize petroleum alkanes. Nature Microbiology (2023). DOI:10.1038/s41564-023-01400-3. [PMC10322722]
growth_records.snippet: Cultures, except the considerably slower C5 culture, doubled within 13-40 d (Supplementary Table 1). Activity doubling times were determined from the development of sulfide concentrations during the first two dilutions.
growth_records.notes: Paper explicitly reports 'activity doubling times' of 13-40 d (excluding the slower C5-alkane culture), derived from an exponential fit (y=n*e^mx) to H2S accumulation during the first two dilution cycles of anaerobic alkane-oxidizing, sulfate-reducing enrichment cultures at 70C. Converted range: 13 d=312 h -> mu=ln2/312=0.00222 /h; 40 d=960 h -> mu=ln2/960=0.00072 /h. Reported here as a range (doubling_time_h=312-960 h, growth_rate_per_h=0.0007-0.0022 /h; single representative slow-end values placed in the numeric fields, full range documented here). This is an 'activity doubling time' (sulfide-production proxy) for a syntrophic archaea+bacteria consortium, not an axenic pure-culture growth rate; no per-gDW substrate uptake or H2S secretion rate could be computed because no biomass (dry weight) data are given.
